source_of_truth: prd/ARCHITECTURE.md (living truth oracle) and .gsd/milestones/* (GSD state). This roadmap is a derived cold-reader summary; when it disagrees with the oracle or GSD state, those win.

completed_milestone_groups.theme: Architecture review, PRD consistency, parser direction, and baseline architecture verification
completed_milestone_groups.boundary: Establishes architecture and parser proof foundations, not production legal-answering readiness.

completed_milestone_groups.theme: GraphRAG, FalkorDB, retrieval, ontology, evidence, and semantic scoring proof cycles
completed_milestone_groups.boundary: Provides bounded proof cycles and diagnostics, not final product retrieval quality or ontology/product readiness.

completed_milestone_groups.theme: Consultant XML source structuring, MiniMax-assisted discovery, graph context staging, and workline recovery
completed_milestone_groups.boundary: Establishes bounded Consultant XML/source workflow evidence, not parser completeness for all legal sources.

completed_milestone_groups.theme: ACP / git-lex reusable-core era — construction, registry integration, RDF projection, then externalization
completed_milestone_groups.boundary: CLOSED. The reusable core was externalized to /root/git-lex-kit-acp/ (published github.com/rager306/git-lex-kit-acp v0.2.0) and law-nexus became a profile consumer (M067, D097). Per D098, this era was itself meta-drift (ACP enforcement ran M035-M063 while the product track stalled); D098 now freezes ACP to checkpoint mode (detect+log+flag, non-blocking) until parser data is ready. Do NOT treat ACP/RDF/SHACL/SPARQL/dashboards as source truth or as the next product milestone.

completed_milestone_groups.theme: Structural foundation crystallization + roadmap freshness guard — onion package, ADR standard, compliance gate (pre-commit + CI), library-boundary contract, anti-drift freshness guard
completed_milestone_groups.boundary: Anti-drift infrastructure only, now fully enforceable locally AND in CI. M068 turned the repo into the src/law_nexus onion package (ADR-0001) with a MADR ADR standard + compliance gate (ADR-0002: import-linter + verify-adr-conformance, wired as hard-fail pre-commit hooks AND as a GitHub Actions CI workflow on push/PR); M069 recorded the library boundary (ADR-0003) and made the standing tree gate-green; M070 fixed the stale project-state roadmap and added a pytest freshness-guard. Does NOT validate R035/R037/R038, does NOT harden the parser, does NOT introduce FalkorDB/graph/retrieval.

current_milestone.title: Roadmap freshness guard
current_milestone.goal: Fixed the stale project-state roadmap (was current=M047, next=ACP — contradicting D098) to the truthful trajectory (M069 complete, M068 needs-attention, parser-hardening next, ACP frozen) and added a pytest freshness-guard parsing .gsd/STATE.md so the roadmap cannot silently drift again.

recommended_next.title: Consultant XML Parser Hardening from M009 baseline (M034 corrected roadmap)
recommended_next.why: Per D098 and the M034 corrected roadmap, the product track resumes at parser hardening. The structural foundation (M068/M069) is laid; the next value is a proof-gated Consultant XML parser on top of the M009 baseline, BEFORE graph/retrieval/R035-R038 work (all of which are downstream-blocked on parsed data).
recommended_next.slices: S01 baseline lock → S02 lxml equivalence eval → S03 structural rules → S04 semantic diagnostics → S05 razdel/pymorphy3 eval → S06 source-span/stable-ID → S07 final proof package
recommended_next.non_validation: Does NOT validate R035/R037/R038; does NOT introduce FalkorDB/graph/vector/retrieval; does NOT claim parser completeness or legal correctness.

downstream_blocked.blocker: needs materialized graph from parsed corpus
needs real EvidenceSpan/SourceBlock fixtures from parsed corpus
needs registry extractor integration + accepted proof-gate evidence (documentation-only is insufficient)
needs production ingest from real corpus

alternatives.title: FalkorDB graph materialization
alternatives.why: Only after parser proof yields a stable record stream; would then exercise the graph-store port and EvidenceSpan fail-closed contract.
alternatives.constraint: Do not start before parser-hardening proof; no R037 claim from synthetic smoke.

alternatives.title: Adaptix boundary-record↔domain-form mapping
alternatives.why: ADR-0003 reserves this as [deferred]; activates only when the parser product wires the boundary-record stream into the domain and mappings become non-trivial.
alternatives.constraint: Premature until parser-hardening produces the consumer.

alternatives.title: ACP unfreeze (observational only)
alternatives.why: Only if a concrete drift is detected+logged OR the user explicitly directs it; D098 keeps ACP as checkpoint (non-blocking), never a gate.
alternatives.constraint: ACP expands ONLY observation, never into a blocking gate; do not re-enter the M035-M063 meta-drift.

frozen.acp: checkpoint mode (detect+log+flag) until parser data ready (D098). Not a milestone track, not a gate.
frozen.r035_r037_r038_validation: not validatable from documentation/projection evidence alone; deferred to downstream product-data milestones.

planning_constraints: Run GitNexus impact before editing code symbols; run GitNexus detect_changes before commits.
Use tracked relative source anchors only (no .gsd/exec, absolute, ignored, or raw-payload anchors).
Keep product/legal/runtime/parser/FalkorDB/retrieval claims bounded unless separate proof exists.
Apply D098 lifecycle tags ([proposed]/[bounded]/[smoke]/[validated]/[deferred]) on architectural/state claims; never smooth a bounded/proposed claim up to validated.
